name: Lionel Messi
hints: Holds the record for most Ballon d'Or wins in history with 8
Spent 17 seasons at a Spanish club before his emotional departure in 2021
Won his nation's first World Cup in 36 years in Qatar
His contract at his boyhood club was famously written on a napkin
Born in Rosario, he was treated for a growth hormone deficiency as a child

name: Cristiano Ronaldo
hints: Has won the Champions League with three different clubs
Set the all-time international goals record, surpassing Ali Daei
His mother reportedly wanted to abort him but was advised against it
He was born on the island of Madeira and supports a club from his hometown
Holds the record for most goals ever scored in professional football

name: Kylian Mbappé
hints: Became only the second teenager to score in a World Cup final after Pele
Rejected a move to Real Madrid for two years before eventually joining
Scored a hat-trick in a World Cup final but still finished on the losing side
Grew up in a suburb of Paris and idolized Cristiano Ronaldo as a child
Donated his entire 2018 World Cup winners' bonus to charity

name: Erling Haaland
hints: His father played for the club he would later break records at in England
Scored 9 goals in a single Champions League group stage match
Set a new Premier League single-season goals record in his debut campaign
Born in Leeds but chose to represent a Scandinavian nation
Follows strict diet and sleep routines, famously wearing blue-light glasses

name: Neymar Jr.
hints: Became the world's most expensive player in 2017, breaking a record by over €100m
Was part of the MSN attacking trio that dominated European football in 2015
Suffered a devastating metatarsal injury in back-to-back World Cups
His former club Santos retired his number 11 shirt in his honour
Brazil's all-time leading scorer, surpassing a record held for decades

name: Kevin De Bruyne
hints: Was deemed surplus to requirements at Chelsea before becoming one of Europe's best
Holds the Premier League record for most assists in a single season (20)
Won the treble in 2022/23 despite missing most of the run-in through injury
Considered by many managers to be the most complete midfielder of his era
Captains the Belgian national side and was part of their 'Golden Generation'

name: Vinicius Jr.
hints: Joined his current club at age 16 in a deal worth €45m — before his debut
Scored the winning goal in a Champions League final in Paris
Has become a prominent spokesperson against racism in European football
His signature skill involves intricate footwork borrowed from Brazilian street culture
Finished runner-up in the 2024 Ballon d'Or in a highly controversial ceremony

name: Mohamed Salah
hints: Was considered a flop at a London club before reinventing himself in Italy
Set a record of 32 goals in a 38-game Premier League season in 2017/18
Won a Champions League title after a famous collision in the final the year before
Africa Cup of Nations winner with his national side
His country's all-time leading scorer and most capped international

name: Robert Lewandowski
hints: Scored 5 goals in 9 minutes as a substitute in a Bundesliga match
Won 8 consecutive top-flight titles at a single German club
Broke a record in 2021 that had stood for 49 years in the Bundesliga
Finished Ballon d'Or runner-up in 2021, with many arguing he was robbed of the win
Was rejected by a Premier League club as a teenager before rising to prominence

name: Luka Modrić
hints: Ended an 11-year duopoly to win the Ballon d'Or in 2018
Was voted the best player at a World Cup where his nation finished runners-up
Survived a kidnapping attempt on his family as a child during the Balkan wars
Was initially deemed too small and frail to make it as a professional footballer
Has won 6 Champions League titles, all with the same Spanish club

name: Harry Kane
hints: England's all-time record goalscorer, surpassing Wayne Rooney's tally
Won the Premier League Golden Boot without ever lifting a major team trophy
Had loan spells at several lower-division English clubs before breaking through
Known for elite penalty-taking and assist-making in addition to goalscoring
Left a north London club for Germany in a deal worth over £100m

name: Virgil van Dijk
hints: Became the most expensive defender in history when signed for £75m in January 2018
Finished third in the Ballon d'Or — the highest-ever finish for a defender at the time
Won the Champions League and Premier League within two seasons of arriving
His club went unbeaten at home in the Premier League across two full seasons with him
Captains the Netherlands and played a key role in their run to the 2019 Nations League final

name: Pedri
hints: Won the Kopa Trophy for best under-21 player in 2021
Played 73 games in a single calendar year as a 19-year-old, causing widespread concern
Regularly compared to a legendary Barça midfielder known for his vision and composure
Born in the Canary Islands and came through a local academy before moving to Catalonia
Was named best young player at Euro 2020 after only a handful of first-team appearances

name: Jude Bellingham
hints: Became the youngest scorer in Bundesliga history during his first season
Scored 23 league goals in his debut season at his current club — extraordinary for a midfielder
Was Birmingham City's starting midfielder at just 16 years old
His father was also a professional footballer who played in the English lower leagues
Won the Champions League in his debut season at a Spanish giant

name: Thibaut Courtois
hints: Was named Man of the Match in a Champions League final with 9 saves — one of the great individual performances
Won the Yashin Trophy for best goalkeeper in 2022, the year of that final
Stands at 1.99m — one of the tallest goalkeepers to ever play at the top level
Won La Liga with Atletico Madrid, the Premier League with Chelsea, and the Champions League
Has been Belgian number one since 2011, winning over 100 caps

name: Lamine Yamal
hints: Became the youngest player ever to appear for Spain's senior national team
Scored a stunning semi-final goal that was directly followed by Spain winning the tournament
Won the Kopa Trophy in consecutive years before the age of 18
A photograph of him as a baby with a future World Cup winner went viral in 2024
Born on 13 July 2007 — the exact day of the tournament final he would later help win

name: Bukayo Saka
hints: Came through the academy and never left a north London club
Won PFA Players' Player of the Year in 2023/24 — the first Arsenal player in 18 years
Can play left back, left wing, and right wing at the highest level
Scored in a Euro shootout final as a teenager, missing the decisive penalty
Was called up to the England senior side at 18 after just a handful of league appearances

name: Florian Wirtz
hints: Was the key creative force in a Bundesliga side that went the entire season unbeaten in 2023/24
Scored the Bundesliga's fastest-ever goal as a substitute when still a teenager
Suffered an ACL tear that ruled him out for 11 months but returned to form immediately
Was coached by a former Champions League-winning captain at club level
Joined a Spanish superclub in 2025 in a deal reported to exceed €130m

name: Jamal Musiala
hints: Was born in Stuttgart but raised in England and trained at a south London academy
Chose to represent Germany over England at senior international level
Became Bayern Munich's youngest-ever Bundesliga scorer
Nicknamed 'Bambi' by teammates for his fluid, agile dribbling
Was one of the standout performers at the 2024 home tournament before a quarter-final exit

name: Rodri
hints: Scored the winning goal in a Champions League final against city rivals
Won the Ballon d'Or in 2024 — only the second Spanish man to do so
Went nearly 80 games unbeaten before a late 2024/25 run ended his club's run
Suffered a serious ACL injury in September 2024 that ended his season
Widely regarded as the finest defensive midfielder in world football

name: Cole Palmer
hints: Was sold by the reigning champions for £40m — widely considered the bargain of the decade
Won PFA Young Player of the Year in 2023/24 after a 22-goal debut season
Scored a penalty in a major tournament final in his first senior international summer
Scored four goals in a single Premier League game against an Everton side in 2024
Lifelong fan of the club that sold him — making his departure feel unexpected

name: Phil Foden
hints: Won the PFA Players' Player of the Year in 2023/24 season
A lifelong supporter of his club who never played for another professional team
Won 6 Premier League titles and a Champions League, all at the same club
Was named man of the tournament at the 2017 U17 World Cup aged 17
Nicknamed 'The Stockport Iniesta' due to his hometown and playing style

name: Declan Rice
hints: Was released as a teenager by Chelsea — who later failed to buy him back for £100m+
Captained a London club to their first European trophy in over 50 years
Joined Arsenal for a then-record British fee of £105m in 2023
Was eligible for the Republic of Ireland before declaring for England
Has evolved from a pure defensive midfielder into a box-to-box goal-scoring force

name: Federico Valverde
hints: Made a famous last-ditch foul to stop a certain goal, accepting a red card to save the tie
Won back-to-back Champions League titles in 2022 and 2024
Nicknamed 'El Pajarito' by teammates and fans
Can play as a central midfielder, defensive midfielder, or right winger at the top level
One of the most physically dominant midfielders in the world by distance covered

name: Trent Alexander-Arnold
hints: Redefined the right-back position as a primary playmaker in the Premier League
Has more assists than any other defender in Premier League history
A lifelong supporter of the club he captained, making over 300 appearances
Delivered a corner kick in the 2019 Champions League semi-final that changed football history
Left on a free transfer to a Spanish giant in 2025 after contract negotiations broke down
